sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to amend Contract No. 32500009 and
any necessary future amendments with Dixon Resources Unlimited for the Police
Department, to include contract implementation support while increasing the contract
amount by $50,000 for a total not to exceed amount of $100,000, and a contract
extension date to January 31, 2027.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Approval of the recommendation would support a smooth transition to a parking citation
and permit management system (CPMS). This is a complex system that impacts the
following Departments: Police, Finance, Information Technology, Public Works, and
Customer Service. The funding for this contract is allocated from the Parking Meter
Fund (631-71-703-812-0000-000-472-612990-).
sections.current_situation:
The Berkeley Police Department currently contracts with Dixon Resources Unlimited for
assistance with preparing a request for proposal (RFP) for the CPMS as well as
management of the project. Dixon Resources current contract began August 01, 2024
and is set to expire July 31, 2025. The Department will require an additional $50,000 in
authorized contract authority to allow the completion of the contract transition to the new
vendor and implementation of the new CPMS contract requirements. Furthermore, this
increase will allow Dixon Resources to provide ongoing implementation support and
help develop a plan among all stakeholders to ensure a seamless transition.
sections.background:
Dixon Resources assists key stakeholders, from Customer Service, Finance, Police,
Public Works, and the Information Technology Departments in developing the
specifications for the CPMS-RFP. The CPMS contract provides citation processing,
collections services, and various customer service resources to support the City. This

contract also assists in support of council directives to implement parking and
enforcement programs in support of the expanded Citywide Residential Preferential
management software. Dixon Resources offers ongoing project management services
to address the City's evolving priorities and ongoing needs regarding the
implementation and deployment of the selected CPMS provider. Dixon Resources will
assist in developing a schedule and training plan for hardware and software changes
and data migration between vendor systems to help ensure the stability and
accountability of the selected CPMS vendor.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or opportunities associated with the
subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
Dixon Resources Unlimited assists with the project oversight of selecting and potentially
transitioning between a parking citation and permit management system contract
through subject matter expert support as well as maintaining milestone deliverables and
ensuring the needs of five City of Berkeley Departments are met. An amendment to
increase funds for the Dixon Resources contract will allow the Police Department to
continue transfer of the CPMS contract to a new system and vendor, if necessary.
CONTACT PERSON
Jennifer Louis, Chief of Police, 981-5700
sections.attachments:
1. Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NUMBER 32500009 AMENDMENT FOR Dixon Resources Unlimited
WHEREAS, the citation and parking management system (CPMS) is complex and
involves five City of Berkeley Departments; and
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley executed contract No. 32500009 for Dixon Resources
Unlimited on August 1, 2024 to help prepare an RFP and manage the CPMS; and
WHEREAS, the Police Department will require an additional $50,000 in authorized
contract authority to allow the completion of the contract transition to the new vendor and
implementation of the new CPMS contract requirements; and
WHEREAS, continuing with Dixon Resources Unlimited will provide ongoing
implementation support to ensure a smooth transition to the selected CPMS contractor.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that, the
City Manager is authorized to execute the contract and any amendments with Dixon
Resources Unlimited to include contract implementation support while increasing the
contract amount by $50,000 for a total not to exceed amount of $100,000, and a contract
extension date to January 31, 2027, subject to the City’s annual budget appropriation
process.
